comprehension_gate.instruction: MANDATORY FIRST ACTION: Before any implementation work, read this entire prompt. Then present a structured summary to the Lead Architect: (a) Milestone objective in your own words (b) List of work items with 1-sentence descriptions (c) Files you will create or modify (d) Test strategy (if applicable — this is primarily a DOCS milestone) (e) Any risks or ambiguities you see Then STOP and WAIT for Lead Architect approval. Do NOT write code, create branches, or run commands until explicit approval is received.

milestone.title: Test Governance Framework
milestone.objective: Create a single authoritative test governance document (docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md) that defines named test scopes with exact commands, scope-selection guidance, the canonical baseline with update policy, marker policy, gate-checking order with pass/fail criteria, and test development policy (when/where/how to write tests). Update the stale test baseline in .github/copilot-instructions.md. Record the milestone in the Post-Operational Maturation Ledger (Entry 38). This single document governs both test execution and test development — no additional documents needed.
milestone.background:
Testing governance is currently fragmented across 10+ touchpoints with no single authoritative document. Phase_2_Test_Plan.md is archived (Phase 2 only). copilot-instructions.md declares a stale baseline of 749/751. Two implicit test scopes coexist (focused vs. full) with no documentation. The slow marker was introduced in M5.5 but no marker policy exists. Different EAs have used different pytest commands with no standard. Root snapshots pytest_baseline.txt (Phase 3, 670 passed) and pytest_output.txt (Task 4, 786 collected) are stale.
The Lead Architect wants: 1. Modular "plug-and-play" test scopes — pick the right scope for the job 2. Consistent governance across all development — every EA uses the same commands 3. No over-engineering — lean document, not a bureaucratic framework 4. Proper baseline management — single source of truth that stays current 5. Test development policy — codify when tests are required, where they go, and how they are structured so the doc governs both execution AND development

item.title: Create docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md
item.description: Create a new governance document at docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md that contains ALL of the following sections. Keep it lean — use tables and exact commands, not prose.

section.title: Named Test Scopes
section.instruction:
Define the following named scopes in a table. Each scope MUST include: - Scope name (uppercase identifier) - Exact pytest command (copy-pasteable) - What it covers (directories/markers) - Expected baseline count (current numbers)
Required scopes:
1. UNIT — Unit tests only for shared library and all services, excluding launcher and integration tests. Fastest feedback loop. Command: pytest shared/ services/ --tb=short -q Current baseline: Derive by running the command (expected ~670-680 based on 755 total minus launcher/ tests). NOTE: The EA MUST run this command to capture the exact count.
2. FOCUSED — Unit tests plus launcher tests, excluding integration. Command: pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q Current baseline: Derive by running the command. NOTE: The EA MUST run this command to capture the exact count.
3. REGRESSION — Full test suite with default marker filtering (slow excluded). This is the standard regression gate for code-change milestones. Command: pytest shared/ services/ tests/ --tb=short -q Current baseline: 755 passed, 2 skipped, 80 deselected
4. FULL — Complete test suite including slow-marked tests. Overrides the default -m 'not slow' filter. Used for task/phase closure gates only. Command: pytest -m "slow or not slow" shared/ services/ tests/ --tb=short -q Current baseline: 835 passed, 2 skipped NOTE: The EA MUST verify this command correctly overrides the addopts marker filter and produces 835 passed. If the -m override syntax does not work, try: pytest -o "addopts=--strict-markers -v --import-mode=importlib" shared/ services/ tests/ --tb=short -q Document whichever syntax works.
5. SERVICE (parameterized) — Single-service test run for targeted development. Command pattern: pytest services/{service_name}/tests/ --tb=short -q List the 5 valid service_name values: policy_agent, assistant_orchestrator, semantic_router, ui_gateway, ui_shell
6. SLOW — Only the slow-marked integration tests. Used for runtime validation. Command: pytest -m slow tests/integration/ --tb=short -q Current baseline: 80 tests (from test_p110_end_to_end.py and test_p114_ui_end_to_end.py) NOTE: The EA MUST verify this command runs exactly 80 tests.

section.title: Scope Selection Guide
section.instruction:
Create a decision table mapping development activity → required test scope. Required mappings:
| Activity | Required Scope | Notes | |----------|---------------|-------| | Single-service code change | SERVICE + REGRESSION | Run targeted first, then regression | | Shared library change | REGRESSION | Shared code impacts all consumers | | Cross-service change | REGRESSION | Any change touching 2+ services | | Code-change milestone gate | REGRESSION | Standard EA quality gate | | Runtime/E2E validation | SLOW or FULL | When testing against live services | | Task closure gate | FULL | All tests including slow must pass | | Phase closure gate | FULL | All tests including slow must pass | | Quick smoke check during dev | UNIT | Fastest feedback loop |
Add a note: "The REGRESSION scope is the default for SDO-generated EA prompts unless the milestone explicitly requires FULL."

section.title: Canonical Baseline
section.instruction:
Define the canonical baseline section with:
1. Current baseline table (one row per scope with pass/skip/deselect counts) 2. Baseline provenance: branch, commit hash, date, ledger entry that established it 3. Update policy: - Baseline MUST be updated when tests are added, removed, or reclassified - The EA that changes test counts MUST update this section as part of their milestone - copilot-instructions.md baseline MUST be kept in sync (reference this doc as source of truth) - Stale baselines are a governance violation - Re-baselining scope rule: When an EA adds, removes, or reclassifies tests, they MUST re-run ALL scopes that could be affected — not just the scope used during development. For example, adding a unit test affects UNIT, FOCUSED, REGRESSION, and FULL baselines. The EA must run each affected scope, capture the summary line, and update EVERY baseline row that changed. This prevents drift where an EA adds tests but only updates one row. 4. Known pre-existing failures: - test_build_prompt_does_not_contain_no_think (2 skipped — pre-existing) - test_stop_token_ids_constants_defined (2 skipped — pre-existing) Document their status and why they are accepted.

section.title: Marker Policy
section.instruction:
Define marker governance:
1. Current markers table: | Marker | Registered In | Purpose | Default Behavior | |--------|--------------|---------|-----------------| | asyncio | pyproject.toml | Async coroutine tests | Auto-applied (asyncio_mode=auto) | | slow | pyproject.toml | Socket-timeout integration tests | Deselected by default |
2. Qualification criteria for `slow` marker: - Tests that require real socket connections with timeout waits - Tests that take >30 seconds individually - Tests that require running services (integration/E2E) - MUST be applied at module level via pytestmark, not per-test
3. Future marker guidelines: - New markers MUST be registered in root pyproject.toml with description - New markers MUST have documented default behavior (selected/deselected) - Adding a marker that changes default selection MUST update the baseline - Marker names: lowercase, single word, descriptive - Any new marker requires a governance doc update
4. Where markers are applied currently: - tests/integration/test_p110_end_to_end.py — pytestmark = pytest.mark.slow - tests/integration/test_p114_ui_end_to_end.py — pytestmark = pytest.mark.slow

section.title: Gate-Checking Order and Pass/Fail Criteria
section.instruction:
Document the mandatory gate-checking order for EA milestones:
1. COMPILE gate: - Command: python -m py_compile {changed_files} OR general import check - Pass criteria: Zero compilation errors - Fail action: STOP — do not proceed to TEST gate
2. TEST gate: - Command: Use the scope defined in the EA prompt (default: REGRESSION) - Pass criteria: a. Zero NEW failures (pre-existing skips are accepted) b. Pass count matches or exceeds baseline for the scope used c. No new skips without documented justification - Fail action: STOP — diagnose and fix before proceeding - Evidence: Capture the pytest summary line in the commit message or ledger entry
3. REGRESSION gate (for milestones that change test infrastructure): - Required when: test files are added/removed/renamed, markers changed, pyproject.toml test config modified - Command: FULL scope (all tests including slow) - Pass criteria: Total pass count matches FULL baseline - Fail action: STOP — the test infrastructure change broke something
Include a note on the relationship to copilot-instructions.md gate-checking order (Compile → Test → Oracle) — this document provides the detailed implementation of that rule.
Add SDO prompt integration guidance: - Every SDO-generated EA prompt MUST specify which test scope to use in its quality_gate section - Default is REGRESSION unless the milestone involves runtime validation or task/phase closure - The EA MUST capture and report the exact pytest summary line

section.title: Test Development Policy
section.instruction:
Define when and how tests are written. This section makes EAs self-service for test development, just as the scope selection guide makes them self-service for test execution. Keep it lean — codify existing patterns, don't invent new ones.
1. When tests are required: - Every code-change milestone that modifies production Python files MUST include tests for new/changed behavior. - DOCS-ONLY milestones and runtime-validation milestones are exempt. - "Production Python files" = any .py file under services/, shared/, or launcher/ that is NOT in a tests/ directory.
2. Where tests go — mirror existing directory convention: - Service tests: services/{service_name}/tests/ - Shared library tests: shared/tests/ - Launcher tests: launcher/tests/ - Cross-service integration tests: tests/integration/ (get the slow marker) - New service → new tests/ directory → update testpaths in root pyproject.toml
3. Naming convention — codify the existing codebase pattern: - File naming: test_{module_name}.py (e.g., test_car.py tests the car module, test_pgov.py tests pgov) - Function naming: test_{descriptive_snake_case} Styles observed in the codebase: * Verb-noun: test_build_car_minimal, test_ssn_detected * State-behavior: test_frozen_immutable, test_construction_defaults * Condition-result: test_elevation_denied_returns_1, test_encode_size_limit_exceeded_raises All names are fully descriptive (no abbreviations), self-documenting, and use snake_case throughout. - Do NOT invent new conventions. Follow the established patterns above.
4. Test boundary rule: - Unit tests (services/*/tests/, shared/tests/, launcher/tests/) MUST mock external dependencies: IPC sockets, file I/O, network calls, hardware interfaces. They run without any running services. - Integration tests (tests/integration/) test real cross-service interaction and MUST receive the slow marker (module-level pytestmark). - No test should require manual setup or external state beyond what pytest fixtures provide.
5. Coverage expectation: - No numeric coverage target (avoids over-engineering). - Gate rule: Every public function or class added or modified MUST have at least one test exercising its primary (happy) path AND at least one test exercising its error/edge path. - This is enforced by code review (SDO or Lead Architect), not by tooling.

item.title: Update copilot-instructions.md baseline
item.description: In .github/copilot-instructions.md, update the test baseline declaration from the stale "749/751 passed" to reflect the current state. The updated text should reference docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md as the canonical source and state the REGRESSION scope baseline: 755 passed, 2 skipped, 80 deselected (FULL: 835 passed, 2 skipped). Also update the HEAD commit reference from 4cdb780 to the current main HEAD (103dfe6) and ledger entry count from 33 to 37.
item.location: .github/copilot-instructions.md, Phase 5 directive block, approximately line 137. Search for "Test baseline: 749/751" to find the exact location. Also search for "HEAD: 4cdb780" and "LEDGER: 33 entries" in the same block.

item.title: Record in Post-Operational Maturation Ledger
item.description: Add Entry 38 to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md documenting this milestone. Follow the existing entry format. Key fields: - Title: Task 6 / Test Governance Framework (COMPLETE) - Date: today's date - Branch: feature/p5-task6-test-governance - Predecessor: Entry 37 (M5.5 E2E Validation, commit 2801db4) - Disposition: PASS - Summary: Created canonical test governance document. Updated stale baseline in copilot-instructions.md. Defined 6 named test scopes, scope selection guide, canonical baseline with re-baselining policy, marker policy, gate-checking order, and test development policy (when/where/how to write tests). - Files: List docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md (created), .github/copilot-instructions.md (modified), docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (modified)

item.title: Note stale root artifacts
item.description: In the TEST_GOVERNANCE.md document, add a brief "Stale Artifacts" section at the bottom noting that the following root-level files are historical snapshots and are NOT authoritative: - pytest_baseline.txt (Phase 3 closure, 670 passed — STALE) - pytest_output.txt (Task 4 era, 786 collected — STALE) - pytest_m53_gate.txt (Task 5 M5.3, 835 passed — superseded by this doc) - pytest_m54_gate.txt (Task 5 M5.4, 835 passed — superseded by this doc) Do NOT delete these files. Just document that they are not the source of truth.

context_notes.note: Root pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options]: - pythonpath = ["."] - testpaths covers: shared/tests, 5 service test dirs, launcher/tests, tests/integration - addopts = "--strict-markers -v --import-mode=importlib -m 'not slow'" - markers: asyncio, slow - asyncio_mode = "auto"

5 service-level configs exist (policy_agent, assistant_orchestrator, semantic_router, ui_gateway, ui_shell). They supplement root config for per-service runs. Content is minimal — mostly testpaths and addopts with strict-markers, or just asyncio_mode.
The workspace has zero conftest.py files. All test configuration is in pyproject.toml. Do NOT create conftest.py files — this is not in scope.

shared/tests/ — IPC protocol, transport, JWT, weight integrity, runtime config (5 files) services/policy_agent/tests/ — adjudicator, boot, car, config_loader, entrypoint, gpu_inference, hybrid_adjudicator + 6 more (13 files) services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/ — circuit_breaker, context_manager, entrypoint, gpu_inference, pgov (5 files) services/semantic_router/tests/ — router (1 file) services/ui_gateway/tests/ — session_store, transport (2 files) services/ui_shell/tests/ — app, pgov_display, streaming (3 files) launcher/tests/ — guest_deploy, launcher, vm_manager (3 files) tests/integration/ — test_p110_end_to_end, test_p114_ui_end_to_end (2 files, both slow-marked)

File pattern: test_{module_name}.py — no exceptions across 32 test files. Function pattern: test_{descriptive_snake_case} — all fully descriptive, no abbreviations, snake_case throughout. Styles: verb-noun (test_build_car_minimal), state-behavior (test_frozen_immutable), condition-result (test_elevation_denied_returns_1). The EA should codify these observed patterns, NOT invent new conventions.

REGRESSION scope (default): 755 passed, 2 skipped, 80 deselected (confirmed 2026-04-18) FULL scope: 835 passed, 2 skipped (from M5.5 pre-streamlining gate) The EA must empirically verify all scope baselines by running the exact commands.

2 tests are skipped (pre-existing since Task 4): - test_build_prompt_does_not_contain_no_think - test_stop_token_ids_constants_defined These are known and accepted. They relate to Qwen3 thinking mode suppression constants that were intentionally deferred.

Line ~137 of .github/copilot-instructions.md declares: "Test baseline: 749/751 passed (2 pre-existing: ...)" "HEAD: 4cdb780. LEDGER: 33 entries (latest: Entry 33 Task 4.11 Security Hardening)." Both are stale after Task 5 completion. Update to reflect current state.

gate.criteria: N/A — DOCS-ONLY milestone. No Python source files are created or modified. Verify that all markdown files are well-formed (no broken tables, no unclosed fences).
755 passed, 2 skipped, 80 deselected. This is a DOCS-ONLY milestone — the regression gate confirms no accidental test infrastructure changes. Zero new failures. Zero new skips.

gate.command: Run each of the 6 named scope commands defined in WI-1 and verify counts match.
gate.criteria: All 6 scope commands execute successfully. Captured counts are recorded in the TEST_GOVERNANCE.md baseline table. REGRESSION and FULL baselines match known values (755/2/80 and 835/2 respectively).
The baseline in copilot-instructions.md matches the REGRESSION baseline in TEST_GOVERNANCE.md. No stale numbers remain.

verification.instruction: The Lead Architect will run these commands to verify milestone success. These are the ONLY confirmation mechanism — the Lead Architect cannot read code.

commands.expected: Sections for: Named Test Scopes, Scope Selection Guide, Canonical Baseline, Marker Policy, Gate-Checking Order, Test Development Policy, Stale Artifacts (or similar headings)
Shows updated baseline (755 passed or 835 passed — not 749/751)
commands.command: python -m pytest shared/ services/ tests/ --tb=short -q --no-header 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 3

commit_template.message:
Task 6: Test Governance Framework
- Created docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md with 6 named test scopes, scope selection guide, canonical baseline with re-baselining policy, marker policy, gate-checking order, and test development policy - Updated .github/copilot-instructions.md baseline from 749/751 to current - Recorded Entry 38 in Post-Operational Maturation Ledger
REGRESSION gate: {EA_FILLS_IN: e.g., 755 passed, 2 skipped, 80 deselected} All 6 scope baselines empirically verified.

attachments.instruction: Attach the following files when creating the EA session:

file.reason: Project governance, coding standards, gate-checking rules
Current pytest config — scopes derive from this
Latest ledger entries for format reference
